task_completion.description: A Geometry Nodes modifier is applied to the 'FencePath' curve.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'FencePath' object is selected in the Outliner or 3D Viewport, and the Geometry Nodes editor is open showing a node tree for it.

task_completion.description: The curve is resampled at exactly 1.5-meter intervals.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Resample Curve' node is present in the Geometry Nodes editor, its mode is set to 'Length', and the Length value is set to 1.5m.

task_completion.description: The 'FencePost' object is instanced on the curve points.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An 'Instance on Points' node is used. An 'Object Info' node referencing the 'FencePost' object is connected to the 'Instance' input of the 'Instance on Points' node.

task_completion.description: The instanced posts are aligned to the direction of the path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Nodes calculating rotation based on the curve's direction (e.g., 'Curve Tangent' connected to 'Align Euler to Vector') are plugged into the 'Rotation' input of the 'Instance on Points' node.

task_completion.description: The procedural fence is visible and correctly generated in the 3D viewport.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D viewport shows multiple 'FencePost' objects distributed along the S-shaped 'FencePath' curve. The posts are evenly spaced and rotated to follow the curve's direction. There should be at least 10 posts visible.

task_completion.description: The project is saved to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Blender application window title bar displays 'procedural_fence.blend', or a save dialog shows the file being saved to '/home/ga/BlenderProjects/procedural_fence.blend'.

integrity.description: The agent used the Blender GUI to construct the node tree.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the progression of adding and connecting nodes in the Geometry Nodes editor, rather than a script being executed in the terminal or text editor.

integrity.description: The fence posts were generated procedurally, not manually duplicated.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Outliner panel does not contain multiple copies of the 'FencePost' object (e.g., no FencePost.001, FencePost.002). Only the original 'FencePost' and 'FencePath' should be present.

integrity.description: The 3D viewport updates correspond to the node tree changes.
integrity.visual_evidence: The instances appear in the 3D viewport only after the 'Instance on Points' node is connected, proving the result is driven by the node setup.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target curve is named 'FencePath' and the instance object is named 'FencePost'. The spacing must be exactly 1.5 meters, which typically yields around 14 instances (minimum 10) along the ~21.2m S-shaped curve. The expected output file path is '/home/ga/BlenderProjects/procedural_fence.blend'.
